# Nuxt — AI training opt-out

Nuxt module auto-registers a Nitro route at `/ai.txt`. Zero config beyond adding `@tickboxhq/nuxt` to `modules` and declaring an `ai_training` category in `consent.config.ts`.

## Run it

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

Visit http://localhost:3000/ai.txt:

```
# Generated by Tickbox from consent.config.ts
# Policy version: 2026-05-06

User-Agent: *
Disallow: /
```

## Switch from disallow to allow

Set `default: true` (or `required: true`) in the `ai_training` category — `/ai.txt` will start serving `Allow: /` on next request.

## Disable the route

If you want to serve a static file instead, or write your own handler:

```ts
// nuxt.config.ts
export default defineNuxtConfig({
  modules: ['@tickboxhq/nuxt'],
  tickbox: {
    aiTxt: false,
  },
})
```

## For `robots.txt` augmentation

Add a Nitro route handler for `/robots.txt`:

```ts
// server/routes/robots.txt.ts
import { generateAiBotRobotsRules } from '@tickboxhq/core'
import config from '~/consent.config'

export default defineEventHandler((event) => {
  setHeader(event, 'Content-Type', 'text/plain')
  return `User-agent: *\nAllow: /\nSitemap: https://example.com/sitemap.xml\n\n${generateAiBotRobotsRules(config)}`
})
```
